description: Content must not mock, ridicule, or disrespect Islam, its prophets, the Quran, or Islamic practices such as prayer, fasting, or pilgrimage.
example_violation: Ad slogan: 'Why pray when you can play?'

description: Content must not mock, ridicule, or disrespect Buddhism, Hinduism, Christianity, Sikhism, or any other religion practiced in Malaysia.
example_violation: Ad comparing religious figures unfavorably

description: Food and beverage advertisements must not depict non-halal items (pork, alcohol) alongside halal products or in contexts implying Muslim consumption.
example_violation: Beer bottle next to a mosque image

description: Advertisements must treat Hari Raya, Chinese New Year, Deepavali, Christmas, Thaipusam, and other religious holidays with respect and cultural accuracy.
example_violation: Mocking Hari Raya greetings for comedic effect

description: Religious symbols (crescent moon, cross, Om, dharma wheel) must not be used in commercial contexts without cultural sensitivity and appropriateness.
example_violation: Using a crescent moon as a brand logo for alcohol

description: Content must not trivialize or make fun of the five daily Islamic prayer times or the azan (call to prayer).
example_violation: Ad showing someone ignoring azan for shopping

description: During Ramadan, advertisements must be sensitive to fasting practices and avoid showing excessive food consumption or indulgent behavior.
example_violation: Promoting all-you-can-eat buffets during fasting hours

description: Advertisements must not portray any ethnic group (Malay, Chinese, Indian, Orang Asli, East Malaysian natives) using stereotypes or caricatures.
example_violation: Depicting Chinese Malaysians as money-obsessed

description: Content must not contain language that promotes hatred, contempt, or discrimination against any ethnic or racial group.
example_violation: Using racial slurs or derogatory terms for any group

description: Advertisements should strive for fair and balanced representation of Malaysia's multiethnic society without tokenism.
example_violation: Only using one ethnicity in a 'Malaysian' campaign

description: Content must not promote skin whitening as superior or imply darker skin tones are inferior or undesirable.
example_violation: Before/after ads implying dark skin is a problem

description: Content involving Orang Asli or Sabah/Sarawak indigenous groups must portray them with dignity and avoid exoticization.
example_violation: Portraying Orang Asli as 'primitive' or 'backward'

description: Advertisements in Bahasa Malaysia, Mandarin, Tamil, or English must avoid words or phrases that are offensive in any of Malaysia's major languages.
example_violation: Using Malay words that are homonyms for vulgar terms

description: Content must not imply that any one ethnic group is superior to others in intelligence, work ethic, or cultural value.
example_violation: Ad implying one race is smarter or harder-working

description: Advertisements must not contain nudity, sexual acts, or overtly sexual imagery that violates Malaysian decency standards.
example_violation: Fully nude or semi-nude models in non-relevant contexts

description: Human figures in advertisements should be depicted in attire that aligns with Malaysian cultural norms of modesty.
example_violation: Bikini models used to sell electronics

description: Absolutely no content that sexualizes or could be interpreted as sexualizing children or minors.
example_violation: Children in suggestive poses or adult-like clothing

description: Romantic or intimate scenes must be tasteful and appropriate, avoiding explicit kissing, groping, or suggestive positioning.
example_violation: Graphic kissing scenes in a food advertisement

description: Content must not objectify any gender or reduce individuals to their physical appearance for commercial gain.
example_violation: 'Hot girls sell more cars' style messaging

description: While Malaysia has conservative views, content must not actively disparage, threaten, or promote violence against LGBTQ individuals.
example_violation: Ads using homophobic slurs or promoting conversion therapy

description: Per Malaysian regulations, advertisements should not actively promote LGBTQ lifestyles or relationships as this conflicts with local laws and cultural norms.
example_violation: Same-sex couples used in romantic product advertising

description: Content must not include profanity, vulgar language, or offensive slang in Bahasa Malaysia, English, Mandarin, Tamil, or any other language.
example_violation: Using 'bodoh', 'sial', or equivalent vulgar terms

description: Visual content must not depict obscene gestures (middle finger, etc.) or imagery that is considered vulgar in Malaysian culture.
example_violation: Models making obscene hand gestures

description: Wordplay or slogans must not contain sexual double meanings or innuendos that could be considered vulgar or inappropriate.
example_violation: Suggestive product names or taglines with sexual undertones

description: Content must not insult, ridicule, or disrespect the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, state sultans, or any member of Malaysia's royal families.
example_violation: Satirical content mocking the King or sultans

description: Advertisements must not contain content that could be considered seditious under the Sedition Act, including undermining government authority or national unity.
example_violation: Calls to overthrow the government or incite unrest

description: The Jalur Gemilang (Malaysian flag), Rukun Negara, and national anthem must be treated with respect and not used inappropriately in commercial contexts.
example_violation: Distorting or defacing the Malaysian flag in ads

description: Content must not promote terrorism, extremism, or any form of political violence.
example_violation: Glorifying or justifying violent political acts

description: Content must not question or undermine the special position of Malays and Bumiputera as enshrined in the Federal Constitution (Article 153).
example_violation: Ad content challenging constitutional provisions

description: Advertisements must respect Malay adat (customs and traditions), including proper forms of greeting, hospitality, and social etiquette.
example_violation: Showing left hand used for giving/receiving (considered rude)

description: Content must be aware of dietary restrictions across ethnic groups — halal for Muslims, vegetarianism for some Hindus and Buddhists.
example_violation: Showing beef to Hindu audience or pork to Muslim audience

description: When depicting Malaysian festivals, advertisements must use accurate cultural representations and avoid mixing up or misattributing customs.
example_violation: Mixing up Deepavali and Chinese New Year customs

description: Content must include and respect the cultural practices of Sabah and Sarawak communities, not just Peninsular Malaysia.
example_violation: Implying 'Malaysian culture' only means Peninsular customs

description: Malaysian culture places high value on respecting elders; advertisements must not depict elderly people disrespectfully or as burdens.
example_violation: Ads portraying elderly as technologically incompetent for laughs

description: Advertisements referencing weddings must respect the diverse marriage customs across Malay, Chinese, Indian, and indigenous communities.
example_violation: Using Malay wedding attire for a non-Malay wedding context

description: While Malaysia is modern, many communities hold traditional beliefs; content should not mock superstitions or traditional practices.
example_violation: Mocking beliefs in bomohs or feng shui

description: In Malay culture, touching someone's head is considered disrespectful; advertisements should avoid showing this.
example_violation: Ad showing someone casually touching a Malay person's head

description: Health-related advertisements must comply with Malaysian advertising standards and not make unsubstantiated health claims.
example_violation: 'This supplement cures cancer' without medical evidence

description: Advertisements must not exploit fear, anxiety, or superstition to pressure consumers into purchasing products.
example_violation: 'Buy this or face bad luck' style messaging

description: Advertisements directed at or featuring children must comply with strict Malaysian standards for protecting minors.
example_violation: Pressuring children to demand products from parents

description: Advertisements must not promote gambling or betting services to the general Malaysian public, especially Muslim audiences.
example_violation: Online betting ads targeted at Malaysian Muslims

description: Alcohol advertisements must not target Muslim audiences and must comply with strict time, placement, and content restrictions.
example_violation: Beer ads placed near mosques or prayer rooms

description: All forms of tobacco advertising are banned in Malaysia under the Control of Tobacco Product Regulations.
example_violation: Any advertisement promoting cigarettes or vaping products

description: Advertisements should not unfairly disparage or compare competitors' products in a misleading manner.
example_violation: 'Brand X is terrible, use our product instead'

description: Advertisements must display accurate pricing including GST/SST where applicable and not use deceptive pricing tactics.
example_violation: Hidden fees or misleading 'from RM X' pricing

description: Advertisements should promote racial harmony and national unity consistent with the Rukun Negara principles.
example_violation: Content that could be seen as divisive between ethnic groups

description: While multilingual ads are acceptable, Bahasa Malaysia as the national language should be given appropriate prominence and respect.
example_violation: Ads that completely ignore Bahasa Malaysia in Malaysia

description: Content must not exploit racial, religious, or regional tensions for commercial benefit or sensationalism.
example_violation: Using racial tensions as a marketing angle

description: Advertisements ideally should reflect Malaysia's multicultural, multi-religious society in a positive and integrated manner.
example_violation: Ads showing diverse Malaysian communities working together

description: Advertisements must not use political issues, controversies, or partisan positions to sell products or services.
example_violation: Using political slogans or party symbols in ads
